event: Satan calling up his legions from the fiery abyss
symbols: Satan standing upright — defiant pride and the refusal to submit to God
golden radiance around Satan — the twisted residue of former angelic glory
fire and lava — the eternal punishment of hell
fallen bodies — the ruined legions who followed Satan in rebellion
dark abyss — the spiritual darkness of separation from God
description: A dark, fiery painting showing a luminous figure — Satan — standing atop a rocky mass surrounded by flames and molten lava, arms raised, calling out to the fallen angels who lie in contorted masses around him. Satan's body glows with a golden-yellow radiance against the surrounding darkness, maintaining a vestige of angelic beauty. Below and around him, twisted, muscular bodies of fallen angels writhe among the flames and dark rocks. The overall palette is dominated by deep blacks, reds, and amber, with Satan's glowing form as the focal point.
theological_description: This vision of Satan rallying his fallen legions draws from Milton's Paradise Lost but is deeply rooted in biblical theology. Scripture describes Satan's fall from heaven: 'How you have fallen from heaven, morning star, son of the dawn! You have been cast down to the earth, you who once laid low the nations!' (Isaiah 14:12). Jesus Himself testified: 'I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven' (Luke 10:18). The scene depicted echoes Revelation's account: 'The great dragon was hurled down — that ancient serpent called the devil, or Satan, who leads the whole world astray. He was hurled to the earth, and his angels with him' (Revelation 12:9). Satan's residual radiance — he was once Lucifer, 'light-bearer' — illustrates Paul's warning that 'Satan himself masquerades as an angel of light' (2 Corinthians 11:14). His defiant posture atop the flames embodies the primal sin of pride — 'I will ascend above the tops of the clouds; I will make myself like the Most High' (Isaiah 14:14). Yet Scripture assures that this rebellion is ultimately defeated: 'And the devil, who deceived them, was thrown into the lake of burning sulfur' (Revelation 20:10).

scripture_refs.reason: The fall of Lucifer from heaven — his former glory and defiant pride depicted in his radiant, standing form
The dragon hurled down with his angels — the fallen legions around Satan
Jesus seeing Satan fall like lightning from heaven
Satan masquerading as an angel of light — his residual golden radiance
The devil thrown into the lake of fire — the fiery abyss depicted in the scene
